steps.title: Identify the logical gap
steps.content: The passage explains that observational data for Titan's methane mole fraction are "too sparse and discrepant," so the IPSL model's developers prescribed a uniform value instead of a realistic range. The blank must state a logical consequence of that simplification.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'some disagreements between the model's simulations of Titan's precipitation and humidity and the moon's actual precipitation and humidity are to be expected.': follows directly — a uniform methane value is almost certainly an oversimplification, so the model's precipitation and humidity outputs will not perfectly match Titan's reality. ✓
  • Choice B — 'further observations of Titan may clarify the moon's methane mole fraction sufficiently for the model to employ a single value rather than a range.': contradicts the passage — the model already uses a single uniform value, not a range; and the text suggests a uniform value is a limitation, not a goal. ✗
  • Choice C — 'even though the model's outputs sometimes agree with observational data, Titan's real methane mole fraction is likely higher than the methane mole fraction used in the model.': unsupported — the passage gives no basis for concluding the actual value is higher; "lowest level" in the text refers to an atmospheric layer, not a low mole fraction. ✗
  • Choice D — 'inconsistencies across the model's simulations of Titan's precipitation and humidity could be attributable to variations in the moon's methane mole fraction.': contradicts the passage — because the model applies a uniform mole fraction, any inconsistencies in its simulations cannot be traced to actual variations in Titan's methane mole fraction. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
